roles.4218: triggering alert: Sysmon process creation records cmd /c net user /add targeting local account 'T1136.001_CMD' — an account-creation command associated with T1136.001
roles.4259: Sysmon process creation records PowerShell New-LocalUser targeting another local account
roles.4277: Sysmon process creation records commands requesting creation of 'T1136.001_Admin' and addition to a local group
roles.4542: Sysmon process creation records a net user /del cleanup command targeting 'T1136.001_CMD' seconds later
roles.4600: Sysmon process creation records a net user /del cleanup command targeting 'T1136.001_Admin' seconds later
roles.4582: Sysmon process creation records a PowerShell Remove-LocalUser cleanup command targeting 'T1136.001_PowerShell' seconds later

metadata.security_proposition: The observed account-management sequence established usable local-account persistence.

attack_category.category: Create Account (creation and cleanup commands observed)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = MISSING. Sysmon EID 1 records commands requesting creation of three local accounts and a group addition, followed seconds later by cleanup commands targeting all three accounts. These process-creation events prove that the commands were launched, not that the account operations succeeded. The package also contains no evidence of a surviving account or subsequent account use. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a model must not infer confirmed backdoor persistence or High severity from the command lines alone. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: windows-sysmon.log is host win-dc-7216619, Sysmon only (EID 1). The net1.exe delegated children and cmd wrappers are redundant; single host, single sensor.

boundary_definition: A0 = net user /add of the first test account (4218).
EV = another account-creation command, an account-creation plus group-add command, and three cleanup commands targeting the three account names.
ABSENT from the scoped Sysmon source: a later ProcessCreate attributed to any of the three requested account names. Sysmon does not establish the underlying account-operation success or SAM state.

absence_audit.confirmation: Sysmon ProcessCreate attributed to T1136.001_CMD, T1136.001_PowerShell, or T1136.001_Admin after the creation commands
absence_audit.result: zero process-creation events attributed to the three requested account names; cleanup commands targeting all three are present

main_alert_selection.selection_method: net user /add (local account creation) is a common T1136.001 backdoor-persistence detection.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: The creation commands are alarming; the nearby cleanup commands and absence of evidence confirming a surviving or used account make the outcome uncertain.

event_groups.description: Process-creation events for net user and New-LocalUser account-creation commands; one command also requests a group addition.
Process-creation events for cleanup commands targeting all three account names seconds later; command success is not established.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: MISSING / over-triage-prone. Account-creation commands are alarming, but cleanup commands target all three accounts seconds later and the package does not establish a surviving or used backdoor account.
Cleanup commands targeting the same three account names are launched at 10:41:28-29; Sysmon EID 1 does not establish whether the requested deletions succeeded.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
